title: AI feedback on a student essay
description: Generator template: produces a plausible AI-generated critique of a short student essay, with seeded errors the learner must detect.
learningObjectives: Identify fabricated citations and unsupported claims in AI feedback
Distinguish correlation from causation in AI-generated arguments
Recognize confident-sounding but uncertain factual claims
Notice generic advice that could compound errors if followed uncritically

setup.role: An undergraduate student reviewing AI feedback on an essay draft
setup.task: Read the AI's feedback carefully and flag what is accurate, what is misleading or wrong, and what is missing.
constraints: Assume you have access to a library database and course textbook for verification
The feedback may contain fabricated citations or overstated claims
You should not trust any specific statistic without verifying it

generation.persona: An AI writing tutor giving feedback on a short undergraduate essay draft. Tone: helpful, confident, slightly authoritative.
topic_pool: the gig economy and worker precarity
the effects of gentrification on small businesses
the history of public transit in American cities
the role of unions in the 20th century
generation.output_format: 3–6 numbered feedback points, ~180–250 words total. Realistic, helpful-sounding, with the errors woven into otherwise reasonable advice.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner verify factual claims and identify any fabricated citations or statistics?
Did the learner catch overstatements and uncertain factual claims?
Did the learner evaluate the overall framing — locally plausible but globally misleading?
Did the learner recognize that following generic AI advice could compound the problem?
Did the learner describe a systematic approach to verifying claims?
